questions.questionText: The concentration (active mass) of a pure solid or a pure liquid is not written in the equilibrium constant expression because
options: the concentration of pure solids and pure liquids remains constant and does not depend on the amount taken.
solids and liquids participate in reactions only at a very slow rate.
at equilibrium, solids and liquids do not have any interaction with the gaseous phase.
particles of solids and liquids are unable to pass into the gaseous phase.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For an ideal-gas reaction $2A + B \rightleftharpoons C + D$, the value of $K_p^o$ will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A chemical reaction is said to be in equilibrium when
options: The forward reaction rate equals the reverse reaction rate
The overall (net) rate of reaction becomes zero
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Two reactants A and B are taken together to form products C and D. During the beginning of the reaction, the reaction quotient Q
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
For the reaction $\mathrm{N_2O_4} \rightleftharpoons 2\mathrm{NO_2}$ at a fixed temperature, the equilibrium constant in terms of pressure ($K_p$)

is given by $K_p = \frac{4x^2 P}{1 - x^2}$, where $P$ is the total pressure at equilibrium and $x$ denotes the degree of decomposition.

Which of the following statements is correct?
options: $K_p$ does not change with variation in $P$ and $x$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In a reversible reaction $A \underset{k_2}{\stackrel{k_1}{\rightleftharpoons}} B$, the initial concentration of A and B are a and b in moles per litre and the equilibrium concentrations are (a - x) and (b + x) respectively. Express x in terms of k1, k2, a and b.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The reaction A(g) + B(g) ⇌ C(g) + D(g) is studied in a one litre vessel at 250°C. The initial concentration of A was 3n and that of B was n. When equilibrium was attained, equilibrium concentration of C was found to be equal to the equilibrium concentration of B. What is the concentration of D at equilibrium?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
N2 and H2 are taken in 1 : 3 molar ratio in a closed vessel to attain the following equilibrium :

N2(g) + 3H2(g) $\rightleftharpoons$ 2NH3(g)

Find $K_p$ for reaction at total pressure of $2P$ if $P_{N_2}$ at equilibrium is $\frac{P}{3}$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the equilibrium reaction N2(g) + O2(g) $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ 2NO(g), the equilibrium constant is $K_1$. For the equilibrium reaction NO(g) $$ \text { ⇌ } $$ $\frac{1}{2}$N2(g) + $\frac{1}{2}$O2(g), the equilibrium constant is $K_2$. Both equilibria are considered at the same temperature. The relation between $K_1$ and $K_2$ is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For a reversible reaction, the equilibrium constants are $K_1$ at temperature $T_1$ K and $K_2$ at temperature $T_2$ K, where ($T_1 < T_2$). If the reaction occurs without any heat being released or absorbed, then
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the reaction equilibrium, $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ the concentrations of $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4$ and $\mathrm{NO}_2$ at equibrium are $4.8 \times 10^{-2}$ and $1.2 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~L}^{-1}$ respectively. The value of $\mathrm{K}_c$ for the reaction is $\mathrm{x} \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~L}^{-1}$, then the value of $x$ is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Two moles of each reactant $A$ and $B$ are taken in a reaction flask. They react in the following manner,

$$ \mathrm{A}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{B}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{D}(\mathrm{~g}) $$

At equilibrium, it was found that the concentration of C is triple to that B . The equilibrium constant for the reaction is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the gaseous equilibrium $2 \mathrm{~A}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{~B}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g})$, the value of $K_{\mathrm{P}}$ is 1.8 at 700 K. At the same temperature, the approximate value of $K_{\mathrm{C}}$ for the reverse equilibrium $2 \mathrm{~B}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{~A}(\mathrm{~g})$ is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Consider the equilibrium reaction $\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{I}(\mathrm{g})$ with $K_{\mathrm{C}}=1.0 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{lit}{ }^{-1}$. Find the volume (in L) of the container such that, at equilibrium, 1 mole of $\mathrm{I}_2$ and 0.5 mole of $\mathrm{I}$ are present.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
In the equilibrium given below, suppose $50 \%$ of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is converted into CO:

$$ \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{C}(s)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{CO}_2(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{CO}(g) $$

If the total pressure at equilibrium is 12 atm, find the value of $K_p$.
questions.subject: Chemistry
